title: WOLVERINE: UNBOUNDED — Game Brief (JSON)
description: Identical JSON conversion of wolf.beastprompt.md. Read exactly once by architect_agent. Never read again after Layer 1.

license.ownership: Game output owned entirely by developer
license.unreal_engine_royalty: 5% gross revenue after first $1M USD per product per calendar year (Epic Games standard EULA)
license.forge_royalty: No FORGE royalty on shipped game

references.traversal: Spider-Man PS4 (traversal fluidity + open world density)
references.world_design: Batman: Arkham City (world design + predator gameplay)

project_metadata.tone: Brutal. Personal. Noir western in a modern city. Wolverine is not a hero. He is a consequence.

the_game.logline: Logan has been living off-grid in a decaying industrial city — part Montreal, part Detroit, part nowhere — when a Weapon X splinter cell begins kidnapping mutants from the streets. Nobody sends him. Nobody asks him. He goes anyway. Over 72 hours the city tears itself apart and so does he.
the_game.design_pillar: The claws come out in the first ten seconds. The game never makes you wait to be Wolverine.

material_responses.flesh: camera micro-stutters, controller vibrates asymmetrically (left claw, right claw separately), audio has weight and wet resistance, enemies react with real physics

pillar_1_you_feel_the_claws.design_rule: The material system is not cosmetic. It is the combat.

pillar_2_damage_is_a_resource.description: Logan takes real damage. Wounds appear on the model in real time. Slashes across chest. Bullet holes. Burn marks. Healing factor closes them visibly over seconds.
pillar_2_damage_is_a_resource.mechanic: Low health = more wounds visible = faster healing rate (body compensating) = higher rage multiplier.
pillar_2_damage_is_a_resource.design_rule: Getting hurt makes you more dangerous. This is not just visual. Damage state affects every system.

pillar_3_the_city_is_alive_and_destructi.description: Open world. Not a sandbox. A living place.
pillar_3_the_city_is_alive_and_destructi.design_rule: Every street fight has consequences that ripple outward. Nothing resets. Destroyed environments stay destroyed. The city remembers what Logan did.

the_basin.description: Starting area — Former industrial waterfront. Rusted cranes, warehouses, container yards. Weapon X is operating here openly. Low-rise, dense, brutal architecture.
the_basin.atmosphere: Night-time rain for first act. Always wet.

midtown_ashford.description: Downtown. Glass and steel over crumbling foundations. Civilians dense. Police presence heavy. Weapon X has infiltrated city government. Mutant underground hides in the subway system below.

the_ridge.description: Elevated residential and campus district. Ashford Biomedical (Weapon X front company) HQ here. Clean surface. Rot underneath. Final act takes place here.

world_scale.traversal: Traversable on foot or via claw-sprint and environment climbing.
world_scale.design_intent: Distance is deliberate — Logan walks through the consequences of what he did.

traverse_times.on_foot: 15-20 minute traverse time across full city

time_of_day_weather.system: Dynamic. 72-hour story plays in real game time (compressed).

gameplay_effects.combat: Weather affects combat (rain slicks surfaces, reduces visibility)

philosophy.application: Every attack chains into the next without stopping. No attack animations that lock you in place longer than necessary. Aggression is always rewarded. Standing still is always punished.

basic_framework.heavy_attack: slow powerful slash, breaks guard, staggers
basic_framework.dodge: short burst in any direction (not a roll — Wolverine doesn't roll, he lurches)
basic_framework.rage: fills from damage taken + damage dealt

combo_system.type: Freeform directional. Not a locked string system.
combo_system.aerial: Aerial attacks available from any jump
combo_system.ragdoll: Enemy ragdoll is physically simulated — where they land affects what you can do next (pin against wall, slam to ground, throw into group)

claw_lunge.effect: claw lunge across gap to enemy
claw_lunge.chain: Can chain: lunge → hit → lunge → hit
claw_lunge.design_intent: This is the Spider-Man traversal equivalent for combat. Crossing a room in three seconds through four enemies via consecutive lunges is the core power fantasy.

rage_system.depletes_when: not fighting (30 second drain if idle)

levels.mid_rage: attacks leave burning claw trails, faster healing

full_rage_berserker_mode.enemy_response: show fear response (some flee, some freeze)
full_rage_berserker_mode.activation: Cannot be manually activated — must be earned through damage

examples: Throw enemy into electrical panel: electrocution
Slam into pipe: impalement (M-rated, not gratuitous — consequence)
Car door: brutal improvised weapon, then discard
environmental_kills.philosophy: The environment is always a weapon.

enemy_types.heavy_units: powered armour, require claw pry to damage
enemy_types.mutant_hunters: counter-mutant tech, can dampen healing factor
enemy_types.sentinels: late-game boss — large, multi-phase, environmental destruction

predator_mode.description: Some missions require no detection. Vent system traversal. Silent takedowns from above. Claw marks on surfaces as environmental storytelling. Enemies find bodies and escalate.
predator_mode.design_rule: Logan is absolutely not stealthy. The point is that he tries. Failed stealth = full combat, which is always an option.

traversal_philosophy.description: Logan does not fly. He does not web-swing. He moves like a predator — low, fast, always threatening. But he is extremely physically capable. The traversal must feel powerful without feeling superhuman-floaty.

movement_kit.claw_sprint: claws out, can slash obstacles/enemies while running, slightly slower but combat-ready
movement_kit.wall_climb: claw into any climbable surface (brick, concrete, metal — not glass), full vertical + horizontal
movement_kit.ledge_vault: automatic when sprinting toward chest-height obstacle
movement_kit.claw_swing: throw claw into overhead surface, swing on it, release to launch — covers 20-30m horizontal (Spider-Man reference — limited version, requires valid surface overhead, not infinite web)
movement_kit.drop: from any height, land on enemy = ground slam
movement_kit.claw_brake: drag claws on wall while falling = controlled descent

traversal_feel_target.description: Moving across the Basin at full speed using claw swing + wall climb + ledge vault should feel like watching a hunting animal move. Fast. Low. Every surface is something to push off from.
traversal_feel_target.references: Mirror's Edge momentum + Spider-Man PS4 fluidity applied to a ground/wall hybrid mover.

progression_system.design_rule: NO SKILL TREES. NO MENUS. Logan does not unlock abilities. He already has them. What changes is Logan's control over himself.

trauma_system.description: Completing missions + finding collectibles unlocks MEMORIES. Memories are not cutscenes. They are playable fragments — 30-60 second first-person flashbacks of past violence.
trauma_system.mechanic: Each memory unlocked = Logan masters one aspect of control.
trauma_system.design_philosophy: This is narrative-mechanical integration: Logan gets better at fighting as he accepts what he is.

weapon_x_intel.description: Files found in the world. Audio logs. Photographs. Each one expands the story of what Weapon X did.

safe_houses.description: 4 locations across the city. Hidden. Logan sleeps. Wounds fully heal. Time advances.
features: No upgrade menus here. Just rest and a journal.
The journal updates automatically with Logan's internal monologue about what just happened. First person, terse.

events: Logan arrives. Finds first mutant victim.
Establishes: what is happening, who is doing it.
Ends with: Logan captured, claws neutralised briefly.
Escape. Logan exposed in populated area.
Meets mutant underground. Alliance of necessity — not friendship.
Weapon X goes public: frames Logan for violence.
Police, media, civilians all against him.
Alone. City locked down. Ashford Biomedical tower.
Everything Weapon X built comes apart from the inside.
Final confrontation: not a boss — a reckoning.
Ends: ambiguous. Logan leaves. City is changed. So is he.

tone_notes: No quips. Logan is not funny in this game.
No redemption arc. He does not become a better person.
Supporting characters die. Some because of him.
The violence has weight because the story gives it weight.
Player should feel effective and uncomfortable simultaneously.

key_characters.dr_reyes: Weapon X lead scientist. True believer, not villain. She thinks she is saving mutantkind. She is wrong.
key_characters.echo: Mutant underground contact. Telepathic. Sees everything Logan has done. Helps him anyway.
key_characters.director_harrow: Weapon X commander. Has the adamantium files. Knows exactly what Logan is. Has a use for it.
key_characters.victor: Shows up at Act 2 end. Not an ally. Not quite enemy. Has his own reasons for being here.

music.style: No orchestral score. No licensed tracks. Reactive ambient score — industrial, percussive, atonal.

states.rage_mode: music drops to pure percussion and distortion

sound_design_priorities: 2. Claw impacts per material (6 material types minimum)
3. Healing: wet cellular sound, subtle, continuous during recovery
4. Enemy reactions: fear escalation through voice
5. City ambience: layered, directional, always present

voice.logan: minimal. Speaks when necessary. Interior monologue in journal.
voice.enemies: reactive to combat state, material, situation.

rendering.lumen: critical for wet surfaces at night
rendering.nanite: high density urban geometry without LOD pop

colour_palette.midtown: cold white fluorescent over dirty concrete.
colour_palette.ridge: false warmth. Corporate beige over clinical white.
colour_palette.rage_mode: desaturation + red vignette (subtle — not comic book)

wolverine_model.costume: No costume for first two acts. Civilian clothes that get progressively destroyed over the 72 hours.
wolverine_model.wounds: visible on model at all times.
wolverine_model.healing: visible: skin knitting, cloth does NOT repair (costume damage is permanent — visual storytelling).
wolverine_model.claws: wet when deployed from flesh. Dry when retracted.

hud_philosophy.health: shown as wound density on model, not bar
hud_philosophy.rage: shown as vignette intensity, not meter (until full rage)
hud_philosophy.objective: compass bearing only, no waypoint beam
hud_philosophy.design_rule: Player learns to read the world, not the HUD.

HR-01.text: CLAWS DEPLOY IN FIRST 10 SECONDS OF GAMEPLAY.
HR-01.note: Tutorial does not exist. The opening IS the tutorial.

HR-02.text: DAMAGE MODEL IS REAL-TIME ON CHARACTER MESH.
HR-02.note: Not a texture swap. Actual mesh deformation for major wounds. Healing is visible mesh restoration.

HR-03.text: NO LOADING SCREENS WITHIN THE CITY.
HR-03.note: District transitions seamless. Only loading: game start, fast travel to safe house (if implemented), mission replay.

HR-04.note: It is earned through damage taken. Never a button press. Any design that makes rage a cooldown = wrong.

HR-05.note: Destroyed objects do not respawn. Ever. The city remembers. Design all areas accordingly.

HR-06.text: CLAW MATERIAL SYSTEM HAS MINIMUM 6 MATERIAL TYPES.
HR-06.note: Flesh, bone, light metal, heavy metal, concrete, glass. Each with distinct audio, visual, and haptic response.

HR-07.note: Every stealth section has a loud solution. Never lock the player out of combat.

HR-08.text: NO SKILL TREES. NO UPGRADE MENUS.
HR-08.note: Progression is entirely through the Trauma System. UI should never show XP numbers, level numbers, or skill points.

functional_requirements.FR-01: Open world city (Port Ashford) — three distinct districts
functional_requirements.FR-02: Seamless traversal: claw sprint, wall climb, claw swing, drop
functional_requirements.FR-05: Rage system: fills from damage, drains when idle, Berserker Mode
functional_requirements.FR-06: Real-time wound + healing system on character mesh
functional_requirements.FR-09: Enemy AI: soldier coordination, fear response, escalation
functional_requirements.FR-10: Predator mode: vent traversal, silent takedowns, body discovery
functional_requirements.FR-12: Dynamic weather system (rain, time of day, lighting impact)
functional_requirements.FR-14: Mutant escalation system (city response to Logan's actions)
functional_requirements.FR-15: Weapon X Intel collectible system (no map markers)
functional_requirements.FR-17: Three-act narrative with playable memory fragments
functional_requirements.FR-18: Reactive audio score (combat intensity → music intensity)
functional_requirements.FR-19: Minimal HUD (wound-based health, vignette rage, compass only)
functional_requirements.FR-20: Haptic feedback system (asymmetric per claw, per material)

non_functional_requirements.NFR-01: 60fps performance mode on PS5 / Xbox Series X
non_functional_requirements.NFR-03: Healing visual: begins within 500ms of damage event
non_functional_requirements.NFR-04: Enemy AI response to player position update: < 100ms
non_functional_requirements.NFR-05: City population density: minimum 40 civilians visible in populated districts at any time
non_functional_requirements.NFR-06: Persistent destruction state survives save/load cycle
non_functional_requirements.NFR-07: Memory fragment load time: < 2 seconds (seamless feel)
non_functional_requirements.NFR-08: Audio mix: claw impact never masked by music or ambience

tech_stack.haptics: Platform-native (DualSense adaptive triggers for claw resistance per material)

execution_rules: Read every phase completely before starting.
Do not skip or merge phases.
Critic returns RETURN TO PHASE [N]: execute immediately.
Every MD begins with SUMMARY block (150 tokens max).
Every MD ends with DECISION_HASH block (JSON).
Phase 6 and Phase 9: read DECISION_HASH first, full content only on contradiction.

tasks: Extract: all FRs, NFRs, HRs.
For each HR: what FORGE test proves it is satisfied?
HR-01: first claw deployment time measured from game start.
HR-02: wound system — define UE5 implementation approach for mesh deformation.
HR-04: rage activation — grep all Blueprint for manual trigger.
HR-08: grep all UMG widgets for XP/level/skill references.
Make every requirement binary testable.
Design: complete UE5 game architecture.
Include GameMode / GameState / PlayerState hierarchy for Wolverine
Character component breakdown: WolverineMovementComponent, ClawComponent, WoundSystemComponent, RageComponent, TraumaSystemComponent
AI architecture: Weapon X soldier BT, fear state machine, escalation manager
World architecture: district streaming, destruction persistence, weather system, time-of-day progression
Audio architecture: MetaSounds reactive graph
HUD architecture: minimal (wound-driven, vignette-driven)
Every component maps to ≥1 FR/NFR/HR.
Build: UE5 module dependency graph.
Nodes: every C++ module, every major Blueprint system, every plugin dependency.

Critical dependencies: ClawComponent depends on MaterialResponseSystem, WoundSystem depends on ClawComponent impact events, RageComponent depends on WoundSystem damage events, TraumaSystem depends on MissionSystem completion events
Cycle detection on module graph. Any cycle = return to Phase 2.
Topo sort → UBT build levels.
Level 0: Core types, enums, interfaces (no game deps)
Level 1: MaterialResponseSystem, WoundSystem (no player deps)
Level 2: ClawComponent, RageComponent (depends L1)
Level 3: WolverineCharacter (depends L2)
Level 4: AI, World, Audio (depends L3)
Level 5: UI, HUD (depends L4)
Critical path: Core → Material → Claw → Character (this is the minimum to get claws in game)
List: EVERY file.
C++ files: .h + .cpp pairs, topo level, FR refs
Blueprint files: name, parent class, purpose, FR refs
Config files: DefaultGame.ini, DefaultInput.ini sections
Include: every UE5 Automation Test file
Include: MetaSounds assets (named, not implemented)
This list is complete and final.

critical_checks: HR-01: does architecture guarantee claw deploy <10s? What is the opening sequence? Is it designed yet?
HR-02: is mesh deformation technically feasible with Chaos Physics + Nanite? Nanite and mesh deform conflict — RESOLVE THIS before Layer 2.
HR-04: is RageComponent state machine truly event-driven with no manual activation path?
HR-05: how does persistent destruction survive level streaming? District reloads — does destruction state persist?
Motion Matching: does it handle wall climb + claw swing transitions? Or does it require custom animation nodes?

tasks: JSON task graph.
First milestone: CLAWS IN GAME — task_ids that get to playable claw combat in empty level. This is the first vertical slice. Target: end of parallel group covering Level 0-3 files.
Second milestone: CHARACTER IN WORLD — Movement kit working in Port Ashford geometry.
Third milestone: FULL COMBAT LOOP — Rage + wounds + material response all functional.
Action: Create ALL folders, C++ header stubs, Blueprint stubs.
C++ stubs: correct UCLASS/UPROPERTY/UFUNCTION macros, correct parent class, correct includes. Zero implementation — pass bodies only.
Blueprint stubs: correct parent class, correct event graph entry points. Zero node logic.
Produce: file count diff. Must match exactly.

phase_9_critic_final.read: ALL Layer 1 MDs + actual folder structure
verify: All C++ stubs pass UHT dry-run (macro compliance).
HR-02 Nanite/mesh deform conflict: resolution documented.
RageComponent: no manual activation path in any stub.
No skill tree / XP / level references in any UMG stub.
phase_9_critic_final.output_format: APPROVED or RETURN TO PHASE [N]. APPROVED = Layer 1 permanently frozen.

tasks: Re-derive at code level.

Critical additions: Motion Matching dataset (what animations needed for wall climb → claw swing → aerial lunge chain?), Chaos Destruction + streaming (exact technical solution for persistent destruction across district boundaries), MetaSounds reactive graph (what parameter drives combat intensity? How does combo count map to audio?), DualSense haptics (does UE5.3 support per-trigger asymmetric haptic strength natively?)

validate: Critical: Nanite + wound deformation conflict — Nanite does not support skeletal mesh deformation. Solution: Logan character uses non-Nanite skeletal mesh. World geometry uses Nanite. Confirm this is Layer 1 error corrected here and document the fix.
Motion Matching traversal: define exact blend parameters.
ClawComponent material detection: line trace per claw tip per frame, or collision event? Which is performant at 60fps?
All UE5 plugin dependencies (built-in vs marketplace).
Produce: list of all plugins required with versions.
Check: Motion Matching plugin availability in UE5.3.
UBT build order at C++ import level.
Critical: WoundSystemComponent tick rate — if healing runs every frame at 60fps on mesh deformation, what is the performance budget? Define tick group and tick interval.

tasks: Full C++ contracts: Every UCLASS (name, parent, key UPROPERTY fields with types), Every UFUNCTION (name, params, return, BlueprintCallable?), Every component (BeginPlay, TickComponent, key events)
Blueprint contracts: Every Blueprint (parent class, exposed variables, event graph entry points: BeginPlay, custom events)

Critical contracts: UWolverineClawComponent::OnClawImpact(FHitResult Hit, EClawMaterialType Material) → void, UWolverineWoundSystem::ApplyWound(FVector Location, float Severity) → void, UWolverineRageComponent::OnDamageReceived(float Amount, AActor* Source) → void (no public ActivateRage function — enforces HR-04)

cross_layer_stress_test: HR-04: UWolverineRageComponent has NO public ActivateRage? Check file_manifest2.md. If it exists = FAIL.
HR-08: search all UMG contracts for XP/level/skill. Must be empty.
Motion Matching: animation clip count sufficient for fluid traversal? Define minimum.
Persistent destruction: solution from architecture2.md correctly reflected in module contracts?

session_milestones: MILESTONE 1 — CLAW VERTICAL SLICE: ClawComponent + MaterialResponseSystem + basic combat. Testable: claw deploy, impact, 6 material responses
MILESTONE 2 — MOVEMENT VERTICAL SLICE: WolverineMovementComponent + Motion Matching base. Testable: sprint, wall climb, claw swing in test level
MILESTONE 3 — DAMAGE LOOP: WoundSystem + RageComponent + healing visual. Testable: take damage → wound appears → heals → rage fills
MILESTONE 4 — ENEMY LOOP: Soldier BT + fear state + escalation manager. Testable: soldier flanks, registers rage, attempts retreat
MILESTONE 5 — WORLD INTEGRATION: Port Ashford geometry + destruction + weather + population
MILESTONE 6 — FULL GAME LOOP: All systems integrated, Act 1 playable

validate: all C++ stubs match contracts in file_manifest2.md.
UHT compile: all UCLASS macros correct.
HR-04 final check: grep all stubs for ActivateRage public function. Must return zero results.

final_sign_off: HR-04 enforced in contracts (no public rage activation).
HR-08 enforced (no XP/level in any contract).
Motion Matching clip list defined and sufficient.
All 6 material types defined in EClawMaterialType enum.
phase_9_critic_final2.output_format: APPROVED or RETURN TO PHASE [N]. APPROVED = Layer 1 + Layer 2 permanently frozen.

phase_10_codingschedule.description: Milestone-driven schedule. Every session maps to one milestone or sub-milestone. Each session has: files, validation method, milestone target. First session: Core types + EClawMaterialType enum + IWolverineInterface. These unblock everything. Build them first.

layer_3_code_execution.reads: Layer 2 MDs + code being written. NEVER Layer 1.

session_pattern.build: implement session N against file_manifest2.md contracts.

HR_check_relevant_to_session.rage_sessions: grep for ActivateRage public → must be empty
HR_check_relevant_to_session.UI_sessions: grep UMG for XP/level → must be empty
HR_check_relevant_to_session.destruction_sessions: verify no reset timer in destruction code

drift_protocol.contract_error: return to layer2/file_manifest2.md — Layer 2 repair from Phase 5
drift_protocol.design_error: return to layer2/architecture2.md — Layer 2 repair from Phase 2
drift_protocol.HR_violation_found: HALT. Return to source phase. Do not patch forward.
drift_protocol.rule: Never Layer 1. Never patch forward past source of drift.

milestones_at_a_glance.M1_CLAW_VERTICAL_SLICE: Claws deploy, impact 6 materials, camera + haptic + audio all respond
milestones_at_a_glance.M2_MOVEMENT_VERTICAL_SLICE: Full traversal kit in test level
milestones_at_a_glance.M8_PLATFORM_GOLD: PC + PS5 + Xbox builds passing cert

layer_2_per_phase.phase_9: ALL L1 + ALL L2 + folder — ~70k ← peak

context_budget.layer_3_per_session: 3 L2 MDs + prev critic + code — ~15-20k ← bounded

model_requirements.layer_1: 64k minimum (game arch is denser than software arch)

summary_compression_rule.every_md_ends_with: ## DECISION_HASH — JSON block with key_decisions, constraints, derived_from, derived_from_hash (sha256)
summary_compression_rule.phase_6_and_9: DECISION_HASH first. Full content on contradiction.

closing: Logan doesn't wait to be Wolverine. Neither does the game.
